old_claim.note: Radiology Departments provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The first therapy linac was installed in a hospital radiotherapy unit and used for patient treatment, making clinical radiology infrastructure enabling context.

source_shape.source_locator: British Institute of Radiology 1950s radiotherapy history section on the Hammersmith 8 MeV linear accelerator.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source says the Hammersmith 8 MeV linear accelerator was installed in 1952 as the first hospital installation and was followed by clinical results publications.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports the radiology-department edge as deployment context because the machine entered clinical use through hospital radiotherapy practice, not as a standalone physics laboratory instrument.

ontology_before: The radiology-department edge was an unsourced generic enabling claim.
ontology_after: The edge remains enabling but is now source-checked against hospital radiotherapy deployment history.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: radiology departments remain an enabling clinical deployment context.
would_reject_if: The node were scoped only to accelerator hardware outside medical deployment.
A source showed the first medical linacs were not hospital radiotherapy devices.
The edge were upgraded to required and confused clinical deployment context with the accelerator component itself.
short_reason: Hospital radiotherapy infrastructure enabled medical linac deployment without being the accelerator mechanism.
